title: erophila verna subsp. spathulata
intro: Erophila verna subsp. spathulata, also known as spring whitlowgrass, is a subspecies of perennial plant in the family Brassicaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and supporting the immune system. Erophila verna subsp. spathulata can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments.
